headline: Marine Le Pen to find out whether she can run for president
subtitle: A court will rule on Tuesday in Marine Le Pen's appeal trial over alleged misappropriation of European Union funds. Its decision will determine whether she can run in the 2027 presidential election.

'A despicable woman unworthy of her office': Kylian Mbappé responds to racist remarks from Paraguayan senator
subtitle: The captain of the French national football team responded on Monday to a racist message posted by Celeste Amarilla. The Paraguayan politician's comments came in the wake of the Albirroja's defeat by Les Bleus in the round of 16 of the World Cup.

headline: As US disengages from NATO, Europe's militaries ramp up scattered and unsynchronized
subtitle: In Ankara on July 7 and 8, European leaders from NATO want to show that they can cope with reduced US involvement and boost their defense capabilities to respond to any attack. While efforts are now underway, Europe is still struggling to coordinate them.

headline: One hundred early works by Claude Monet reunited at Le Havre's André Malraux Museum
subtitle: The MuMa exhibition, showcasing drawings, caricatures and paintings, places the painter in his family and regional context.

headline: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subtitle: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

subtitle: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

headline: Resigned Le Pen puts on brave face before crucial court decision
subtitle: In her final public appearance before the ruling on Tuesday that will determine whether she can run for president, Marine Le Pen focused on highlighting her partnership with Jordan Bardella.

headline: 'What comes next will be just as grueling': Villagers remain anxious after wildfire in southern France
subtitle: A wildfire fanned by fierce winds amid extreme heat consumed 540 hectares of scrubland in the Gard department within a matter of hours.
